references.title: Labor Market Impacts of AI: A New Measure and Early Evidence
references.relevance: Introduces 'observed exposure' measure combining theoretical LLM capability with real usage data. Finds no systematic unemployment increase for exposed workers, but suggestive 14% drop in hiring rate for workers aged 22-25 in exposed occupations. Key anchor for the 'revolution happening in a phone booth' argument.

references.title: The Tipping Point: How Little Things Can Make a Big Difference
references.relevance: Law of the Few — Connectors, Mavens, Salesmen. 80/20 principle. Framework for how a small number of actors drive outsized outcomes. Applied to AI power users as productivity super-emitters.

references.title: Revenge of the Tipping Point: Overstories, Superspreaders, and the Rise of Social Engineering
references.relevance: Extends Law of the Few to 'Law of the Very, Very, Very Few.' Stedman's car pollution study (5% of cars = 55% of pollution). COVID super-spreader research (86% of virus particles from 2 of 36 volunteers). Overstories and monoculture fragility concepts. Anchor metaphor for entire article.

references.title: Intuition to Evidence: Multi-Agent PR Review (DeputyDev Study)
references.relevance: 300 engineers tracked over 1 year. Top 30 high-adoption users: +61% shipped code. Bottom 30: -11% decline. 31.8% overall efficiency gain. Cost: ~$31/engineer/month. Proves power law of adoption intensity.

references.title: METR Study — Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI
references.relevance: Randomized controlled trial: 16 experienced open-source devs, 246 tasks. Devs predicted 24% speedup, perceived 20% speedup, but were actually 19% SLOWER. 40-percentage-point perception gap. Key evidence for 'cognitive super-spreading' argument.

references.title: GitClear 2025 Code Quality Analysis
references.relevance: 150M+ lines of code analyzed. Copy-pasted code: 8.3% → 12.3% (+48%). Refactoring plummeted 60% (25% → <10%). Code churn projected to double. Evidence for AI damage super-emitters.

references.title: Veracode 2025 AI Code Security Study
references.relevance: 45% of AI-generated code fails security tests. Java failure rate: 72%. Evidence for systemic risk from AI-assisted development.

references.title: BCG 10-20-70 Rule and Cost Transformation Analysis
references.relevance: 10% algorithms, 20% technology, 70% workflow redesign. Only 21% of companies redesign workflows. Cost-reduction framing: 50% hit targets. Revenue-growth framing: 63% hit targets.

references.title: Daniel Stenberg — AI Slop and Open Source
references.relevance: Warned that 'AI slop is DDoSing open source' — maintainers flooded with superficially correct but deeply flawed AI-generated PRs.

references.title: Stanford University — Youth Employment in Software Development
references.relevance: 20% decline in employment for software devs aged 22-25 between late 2022 and July 2025. Entry-level positions declined 60% since 2022.
